message.content:
I need to understand the UX/interaction patterns of these three professional CLIs: Claude Code (claude),  CLI (), and Codex CLI (codex). 

Please look at:
1. `/home/mega/.local/bin/` or `which claude`, `which `, `which codex` - find their actual executables if present
2. Any source files or scripts for these CLIs on the system
3. Look in `/home/mega/.claude/`, `/home/mega/./`, `/home/mega/.codex/` for config/source clues

I specifically want to know:
- How they display responses (streaming token by token? markdown rendered? plain text?)
- How the input prompt looks (readline? what prefix/prompt symbol?)  
- Do they show a spinner/loading indicator while waiting for response?
- How is the banner/startup look?
- What commands/keybindings do they support?
- Overall layout and color scheme

Report what you find. Research only, no code changes.

content.text: I'll help you understand the UX/interaction patterns of these three CLIs. Let me start by exploring the system to find their executables and configuration files.

content.content:
/home/mega/.local/bin/claude
/home/mega/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin/
/home/mega/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin/codex

content.text: Now let me look at Codex more closely and also check for example CLI output or README files:

content.content:
1

npm i -g @openai/codex
or brew install --cask codex

2

Codex CLI is a coding agent from OpenAI that runs locally on your computer. 3

4 5

6
7 If you want Codex in your code editor (VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf), install in your IDE. 8
If you want the desktop app experience, run codex app or visit the Codex App page. 9
If you are looking for the cloud-based agent from OpenAI, Codex Web, go to chatgpt.com/codex.

10 11 --- 12 13 ## Quickstart 14 15 ### Installing and running Codex CLI 16 17 Install globally with your preferred package manager: 18 19 ```shell 20 # Install using npm 21 npm install -g @openai/codex 22 ``` 23 24 ```shell 25 # Install using Homebrew 26 brew install --cask codex 27 ``` 28 29 Then simply run `codex` to get started. 30 31
32 You can also go to the latest GitHub Release and download the appropriate binary for your platform. 33 34 Each GitHub Release contains many executables, but in practice, you likely want one of these: 35 36 - macOS 37 - Apple Silicon/arm64: `codex-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz` 38 - x86_64 (older Mac hardware): `codex-x86_64-apple-darwin.tar.gz` 39 - Linux 40 - x86_64: `codex-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz` 41 - arm64: `codex-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz` 42 43 Each archive contains a single entry with the platform baked into the name (e.g., `codex-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl`), so you likely want to rename it to `codex` after extracting it. 44 45
46 47 ### Using Codex with your ChatGPT plan 48 49 Run `codex` and select **Sign in with ChatGPT**. We recommend signing into your ChatGPT account to use Codex as part of your Plus, Pro, Team, Edu, or Enterprise plan. [Learn more about what's included in your ChatGPT plan](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/11369540-codex-in-chatgpt). 50 51 You can also use Codex with an API key, but this requires [additional setup](https://developers.openai.com/codex/auth#sign-in-with-an-api-key). 52 53 ## Docs 54 55 - [**Codex Documentation**](https://developers.openai.com/codex) 56 - [**Contributing**](./docs/contributing.md) 57 - [**Installing & building**](./docs/install.md) 58 - [**Open source fund**](./docs/open-source-fund.md) 59 60 This repository is licensed under the [Apache-2.0 License](LICENSE). 61 Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1 # CLI 2 3 [![ CLI CI](https://github.com/google-/-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/google-/-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml) 4 [![ CLI E2E (Chained)](https://github.com/google-/-cli/actions/workflows/chained_e2e.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/google-/-cli/actions/workflows/chained_e2e.yml) 5 [![Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@google/-cli)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@google/-cli) 6 [![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/google-/-cli)](https://github.com/google-/-cli/blob/main/LICENSE) 7 [![View Code Wiki](https://assets.codewiki.google/readme-badge/static.svg)](https://codewiki.google/github.com/google-/-cli?utm_source=badge&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=github.com/google-/-cli) 8 9 ![ CLI Screenshot](/docs/assets/-screenshot.png) 10 11 CLI is an open-source AI agent that brings the power of directly 12 into your terminal. It provides lightweight access to , giving you the 13 most direct path from your prompt to our model. 14 15 Learn all about CLI in our [documentation](https://cli.com/docs/). 16 17 ## 🚀 Why CLI? 18 19 - **🎯 Free tier**: 60 requests/min and 1,000 requests/day with personal Google 20 account. 21 - **🧠 Powerful 3 models**: Access to improved reasoning and 1M token 22 context window. 23 - **🔧 Built-in tools**: Google Search grounding, file operations, shell 24 commands, web fetching. 25 - **🔌 Extensible**: MCP (Model Context Protocol) support for custom 26 integrations. 27 - **💻 Terminal-first**: Designed for developers who live in the command line. 28 - **🛡️ Open source**: Apache 2.0 licensed. 29 30 ## 📦 Installation 31 32 See 33 [ CLI installation, execution, and releases](./docs/get-started/installation.md) 34 for recommended system specifications and a detailed installation guide. 35 36 ### Quick Install 37 38 #### Run instantly with npx 39 40 ```bash 41 # Using npx (no installation required) 42 npx @google/-cli 43 ``` 44 45 #### Install globally with npm 46 47 ```bash 48 npm install -g @google/-cli 49 ``` 50 51 #### Install globally with Homebrew (macOS/Linux) 52 53 ```bash 54 brew install -cli 55 ``` 56 57 #### Install globally with MacPorts (macOS) 58 59 ```bash 60 sudo port install -cli 61 ``` 62 63 #### Install with Anaconda (for restricted environments) 64 65 ```bash 66 # Create and activate a new environment 67 conda create -y -n _env -c conda-forge nodejs 68 conda activate _env 69 70 # Install CLI globally via npm (inside the environment) 71 npm install -g @google/-cli 72 ``` 73 74 ## Release Cadence and Tags 75 76 See [Releases](./docs/releases.md) for more details. 77 78 ### Preview 79 80 New preview releases will be published each week at UTC 23:59 on Tuesdays. These 81 releases will not have been fully vetted and may contain regressions or other 82 outstanding issues. Please help us test and install with `preview` tag. 83 84 ```bash 85 npm install -g @google/-cli@preview 86 ``` 87 88 ### Stable 89 90 - New stable releases will be published each week at UTC 20:00 on Tuesdays, this 91 will be the full promotion of last week's `preview` release + any bug fixes 92 and validations. Use `latest` tag. 93 94 ```bash 95 npm install -g @google/-cli@latest 96 ``` 97 98 ### Nightly 99 100 - New releases will be published each day at UTC 00:00. This will be all changes 101 from the main branch as represented at time of release. It should be assumed 102 there are pending validations and issues. Use `nightly` tag. 103 104 ```bash 105 npm install -g @google/-cli@nightly 106 ``` 107 108 ## 📋 Key Features 109 110 ### Code Understanding & Generation 111 112 - Query and edit large codebases 113 - Generate new apps from PDFs, images, or sketches using multimodal capabilities 114 - Debug issues and troubleshoot with natural language 115 116 ### Automation & Integration 117 118 - Automate operational tasks like querying pull requests or handling complex 119 rebases 120 - Use MCP servers to connect new capabilities, including 121 [media generation with Imagen, Veo or Lyria](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/vertex-ai-creative-studio/tree/main/experiments/mcp-genmedia) 122 - Run non-interactively in scripts for workflow automation 123 124 ### Advanced Capabilities 125 126 - Ground your queries with built-in 127 [Google Search](https://ai.google.dev/-api/docs/grounding) for real-time 128 information 129 - Conversation checkpointing to save and resume complex sessions 130 - Custom context files (.md) to tailor behavior for your projects 131 132 ### GitHub Integration 133 134 Integrate CLI directly into your GitHub workflows with 135 [** CLI GitHub Action**](https://github.com/google-github-actions/run--cli): 136 137 - **Pull Request Reviews**: Automated code review with contextual feedback and 138 suggestions 139 - **Issue Triage**: Automated labeling and prioritization of GitHub issues based 140 on content analysis 141 - **On-demand Assistance**: Mention `@-cli` in issues and pull requests 142 for help with debugging, explanations, or task delegation 143 - **Custom Workflows**: Build automated, scheduled and on-demand workflows 144 tailored to your team's needs 145 146 ## 🔐 Authentication Options 147 148 Choose the authentication method that best fits your needs: 149 150 ### Option 1: Sign in with Google (OAuth login using your Google Account) 151 152 **✨ Best for:** Individual developers as well as anyone who has a Code 153 Assist License. (see 154 [quota limits and terms of service](https://cloud.google.com//docs/quotas) 155 for details) 156 157 **Benefits:** 158 159 - **Free tier**: 60 requests/min and 1,000 requests/day 160 - ** 3 models** with 1M token context window 161 - **No API key management** - just sign in with your Google account 162 - **Automatic updates** to latest models 163 164 #### Start CLI, then choose _Sign in with Google_ and follow the browser authentication flow when prompted 165 166 ```bash 167 168 ``` 169 170 #### If you are using a paid Code Assist License from your organization, remember to set the Google Cloud Project 171 172 ```bash 173 # Set your Google Cloud Project 174 export GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT="YOUR_PROJECT_ID" 175 176 ``` 177 178 ### Option 2: API Key 179 180 **✨ Best for:** Developers who need specific model control or paid tier access 181 182 **Benefits:** 183 184 - **Free tier**: 1000 requests/day with 3 (mix of flash and pro) 185 - **Model selection**: Choose specific models 186 - **Usage-based billing**: Upgrade for higher limits when needed 187 188 ```bash 189 # Get your key from https://aistudio.google.com/apikey 190 export _API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY" 191 192 ``` 193 194 ### Option 3: Vertex AI 195 196 **✨ Best for:** Enterprise teams and production workloads 197 198 **Benefits:** 199 200 - **Enterprise features**: Advanced security and compliance 201 - **Scalable**: Higher rate limits with billing account 202 - **Integration**: Works with existing Google Cloud infrastructure 203 204 ```bash 205 # Get your key from Google Cloud Console 206 export GOOGLE_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY" 207 export GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=true 208 209 ``` 210 211 For Google Workspace accounts and other authentication methods, see the 212 [authentication guide](./docs/get-started/authentication.md). 213 214 ## 🚀 Getting Started 215 216 ### Basic Usage 217 218 #### Start in current directory 219 220 ```bash 221 222 ``` 223 224 #### Include multiple directories 225 226 ```bash 227 --include-directories ../lib,../docs 228 ``` 229 230 #### Use specific model 231 232 ```bash 233 -m -2.5-flash 234 ``` 235 236 #### Non-interactive mode for scripts 237 238 Get a simple text response: 239 240 ```bash 241 -p "Explain the architecture of this codebase" 242 ``` 243 244 For more advanced scripting, including how to parse JSON and handle errors, use 245 the `--output-format json` flag to get structured output: 246 247 ```bash 248 -p "Explain the architecture of this codebase" --output-format json 249 ``` 250 251 For real-time event streaming (useful for monitoring long-running operations), 252 use `--output-format stream-json` to get newline-delimited JSON events: 253 254 ```bash 255 -p "Run tests and deploy" --output-format stream-json 256 ``` 257 258 ### Quick Examples 259 260 #### Start a new project 261 262 ```bash 263 cd new-project/ 264 265 > Write me a Discord bot that answers questions using a FAQ.md file I will provide 266 ``` 267 268 #### Analyze existing code 269 270 ```bash 271 git clone https://github.com/google-/-cli 272 cd -cli 273 274 > Give me a summary of all of the changes that went in yesterday 275 ``` 276 277 ## 📚 Documentation 278 279 ### Getting Started 280 281 - [**Quickstart Guide**](./docs/get-started/index.md) - Get up and running 282 quickly. 283 - [**Authentication Setup**](./docs/get-started/authentication.md) - Detailed 284 auth configuration. 285 - [**Configuration Guide**](./docs/reference/configuration.md) - Settings and 286 customization. 287 - [**Keyboard Shortcuts**](./docs/reference/keyboard-shortcuts.md) - 288 Productivity tips. 289 290 ### Core Features 291 292 - [**Commands Reference**](./docs/reference/commands.md) - All slash commands 293 (`/help`, `/chat`, etc). 294 - [**Custom Commands**](./docs/cli/custom-commands.md) - Create your own 295 reusable commands. 296 - [**Context Files (.md)**](./docs/cli/-md.md) - Provide persistent 297 context to CLI. 298 - [**Checkpointing**](./docs/cli/checkpointing.md) - Save and resume 299 conversations. 300 - [**Token Caching**](./docs/cli/token-caching.md) - Optimize token usage. 301 302 ### Tools & Extensions 303 304 - [**Built-in Tools Overview**](./docs/reference/tools.md) 305 - [File System Operations](./docs/tools/file-system.md) 306 - [Shell Commands](./docs/tools/shell.md) 307 - [Web Fetch & Search](./docs/tools/web-fetch.md) 308 - [**MCP Server Integration**](./docs/tools/mcp-server.md) - Extend with custom 309 tools. 310 - [**Custom Extensions**](./docs/extensions/index.md) - Build and share your own 311 commands. 312 313 ### Advanced Topics 314 315 - [**Headless Mode (Scripting)**](./docs/cli/headless.md) - Use CLI in 316 automated workflows. 317 - [**Architecture Overview**](./docs/architecture.md) - How CLI works. 318 - [**IDE Integration**](./docs/ide-integration/index.md) - VS Code companion. 319 - [**Sandboxing & Security**](./docs/cli/sandbox.md) - Safe execution 320 environments. 321 - [**Trusted Folders**](./docs/cli/trusted-folders.md) - Control execution 322 policies by folder. 323 - [**Enterprise Guide**](./docs/cli/enterprise.md) - Deploy and manage in a 324 corporate environment. 325 - [**Telemetry & Monitoring**](./docs/cli/telemetry.md) - Usage tracking. 326 - [**Tools reference**](./docs/reference/tools.md) - Built-in tools overview. 327 - [**Local development**](./docs/local-development.md) - Local development 328 tooling. 329 330 ### Troubleshooting & Support 331 332 - [**Troubleshooting Guide**](./docs/resources/troubleshooting.md) - Common 333 issues and solutions. 334 - [**FAQ**](./docs/resources/faq.md) - Frequently asked questions. 335 - Use `/bug` command to report issues directly from the CLI. 336 337 ### Using MCP Servers 338 339 Configure MCP servers in `~/./settings.json` to extend CLI with 340 custom tools: 341 342 ```text 343 > @github List my open pull requests 344 > @slack Send a summary of today's commits to #dev channel 345 > @database Run a query to find inactive users 346 ``` 347 348 See the [MCP Server Integration guide](./docs/tools/mcp-server.md) for setup 349 instructions. 350 351 ## 🤝 Contributing 352 353 We welcome contributions! CLI is fully open source (Apache 2.0), and we 354 encourage the community to: 355 356 - Report bugs and suggest features. 357 - Improve documentation. 358 - Submit code improvements. 359 - Share your MCP servers and extensions. 360 361 See our [Contributing Guide](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for development setup, coding 362 standards, and how to submit pull requests. 363 364 Check our [Official Roadmap](https://github.com/orgs/google-/projects/11) 365 for planned features and priorities. 366 367 ## 📖 Resources 368 369 - **[Official Roadmap](./ROADMAP.md)** - See what's coming next. 370 - **[Changelog](./docs/changelogs/index.md)** - See recent notable updates. 371 - **[NPM Package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@google/-cli)** - Package 372 registry. 373 - **[GitHub Issues](https://github.com/google-/-cli/issues)** - 374 Report bugs or request features. 375 - **[Security Advisories](https://github.com/google-/-cli/security/advisories)** - 376 Security updates. 377 378 ### Uninstall 379 380 See the [Uninstall Guide](./docs/resources/uninstall.md) for removal 381 instructions. 382 383 ## 📄 Legal 384 385 - **License**: [Apache License 2.0](LICENSE) 386 - **Terms of Service**: [Terms & Privacy](./docs/resources/tos-privacy.md) 387 - **Security**: [Security Policy](SECURITY.md) 388 389 --- 390 391

392 Built with ❤️ by Google and the open source community 393

394 Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report on the UX/interaction patterns of these three CLIs.

## UX/Interaction Patterns Analysis: Claude CLI vs  CLI vs Codex CLI

Based on my examination of the executables, source code, configuration files, and UI components, here's what I found:

### **1. CLAUDE CODE (claude)**
**Location:** `/home/mega/.local/bin/claude` -> `/home/mega/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.87` (binary executable)

**Display Mechanism:**
- Native binary application (ELF x86-64 executable, not JavaScript-based)
- Likely uses custom rendering/TUI framework
- Settings stored in `/home/mega/.claude/settings.json`
- Rich plugin ecosystem in `/home/mega/.claude/skills/` (63 directories)

**Input Style:**
- Interactive CLI with readline support
- Likely uses custom input handling
- Supports MCP servers for extensibility
- Command-based interface with permissions system

**Spinner/Loading:**
- Managed through the binary application

**Startup/Banner:**
- Custom binary implementation

**Commands/Keybindings:**
- Extensive command system with ~63 installed skills
- Supports `claude --help` and various subcommands
- MCP server integration support
- Settings allow high autonomy mode with peakMode=true

**Color Scheme & Layout:**
- Configured in settings with plugins enabled
- Supports voice interaction (voiceEnabled: true)
- Visual configuration is binary-dependent

---

### **2.  CLI ()**
**Location:** `/home/mega/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin/` -> `@google/-cli/dist/index.js`

**Display Mechanism:**
- **React + Ink framework** for terminal UI rendering
- Interactive mode uses `ink` library for React-based TUI
- Non-interactive mode outputs plain text via TextOutput class
- Markdown rendering support built-in (`renderMarkdown` setting)
- Streaming token-by-token output via StreamJsonFormatter

**Input Style:**
- Interactive mode: React-based Composer component with InputPrompt
- Input history support via `useInputHistory` hook
- Multiline input support with autocomplete suggestions
- Supports both interactive and non-interactive (piped) modes
- Readline-based stdin handling in non-interactive mode

**Spinner/Loading Indicators:**
- Custom **RespondingSpinner** component with spinner types ('dots' by default)
- Visual state tracking via StreamingState enum (Idle, Responding, etc.)
- Supports screen reader mode with text fallback (SCREEN_READER_RESPONDING)
- Shows "Cancelling..." message on Ctrl+C (200ms delay)

**Startup/Banner:**
- Dynamic window title support with folder name
- Initial state before React starts: writes ANSI escape codes for terminal title
- Optional startup warnings based on context
- "Alternate buffer mode" for full-screen terminal usage

**Commands/Keybindings:**
- `/help`, `/chat`, `/bug` slash commands
- Vim mode support (useVimMode hook)
- Mouse events enabled in alternate buffer mode
- Keyboard shortcuts documented at `https://cli.com/docs/cli/keyboard-shortcuts/`
- Customizable key bindings via key matchers and Kitty keyboard protocol support
- Configuration in `/home/mega/./settings.json` (minimal, auth-only)

**Color Scheme & Layout:**
- **Theme manager** with semantic color support (Colors.js)
- Colors include: LightBlue, AccentPurple, AccentCyan, AccentGreen, AccentYellow, AccentRed
- Diff colors (DiffAdded, DiffRemoved), Comment color, Gray
- Message/Input background colors with opacity settings
- Tool status symbols: ✓ (success), o (pending), ⊷ (executing), ? (confirming), - (cancelled), x (error)
- Layout components: MainContent, DialogManager, Composer, Notifications
- Supports narrow terminal widths with adaptive UI (MIN_TERMINAL_WIDTH_FOR_FULL_LABEL = 100)
- Overflow handling and scrollbar rendering in alternate buffer
- Max message lines limit: 65,536

---

### **3. CODEX CLI (codex)**
**Location:** `/home/mega/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin/codex` -> Node.js wrapper -> Platform-specific binary

**Architecture:**
- Wrapper script that delegates to platform-specific vendored binary
- Supports multiple platforms: Linux (x64/arm64), macOS (x64/arm64), Windows (x64/arm64)
- Binary packages from `@openai/codex-*` npm packages
- Configuration in `/home/mega/.codex/config.toml` (TOML format)
- SQLite databases for logging and state management (`logs_1.sqlite`, `state_5.sqlite`)

**Display Mechanism:**
- Appears to be a native binary application (platform-specific executables)
- Custom rendering framework (not React/Ink)
- MCP servers configured in config.toml (filesystem, github-local, sequential-thinking, fetch, ollama, git, memory, Neon, Notion)
- Plugin system with OpenAI-curated plugins: google-calendar, gmail, canva, vercel, box, github, google-drive

**Input Style:**
- Interactive TUI interface (native binary)
- Custom readline/input handling
- Non-interactive and app modes available

**Spinner/Loading:**
- Native binary implementation

**Startup/Banner:**
- Personality system (set to "pragmatic")
- System instructions define behavior

**Commands/Keybindings:**
- Custom command handlers
- MCP server integration
- Approved plugins system

**Configuration:**
- Theme setting: "dracula" theme
- Approval policy: "on-request"
- Model: gpt-5.4 with low reasoning effort
- Sandbox mode: "workspace-write"
- JS REPL enabled
- Guardian approval enabled
- TUI app server enabled

**Color Scheme & Layout:**
- Dracula theme configured
- Configured via `[tui]` section in TOML

---

### **Key Differences Summary**

| Aspect | Claude |  | Codex |
|--------|--------|--------|-------|
| **Tech Stack** | Native binary | React + Ink | Platform binary wrapper |
| **Response Display** | Binary-based | Token streaming with Ink UI | Native binary |
| **Input Method** | Custom binary | React Composer + Ink | Native TUI |
| **Spinner** | Binary-dependent | RespondingSpinner (visual + text) | Binary-dependent |
| **Config Format** | JSON | JSON (minimal) | TOML |
| **Theme** | Plugin-based | Semantic color theme manager | Dracula (TOML-config) |
| **Keybindings** | Custom | Keymatchers + Vim mode | Binary-configured |
| **Terminal Mode** | Standard | Alternate buffer + standard | Standard |
| **MCP Support** | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| **Color Support** | Rich plugin system | Semantic colors + opacity | Dracula theme |
